content.subtitle: Use
rows: To say that one person, thing or action differs from another

The city centre is **more crowded than** the suburbs.
Could you speak a little **more slowly**, please?

content.subtitle: Form: adjectives
rows: one syllable ending in vowel + consonant

content.subtitle: Form: adverbs
rows: well → **better**, badly → **worse**, early → **earlier**, near → **nearer**, late → **later**, fast → **faster**, far → **farther/further**

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: The comparative is usually followed by **than**: *This route is **longer than** the one we took before.*

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To identify the one member of a group that has the most or least of a quality

This is **the most popular** café in the whole city.
Of all the runners, she finished **the fastest**.

content.subtitle: Form: adjectives
rows: one syllable ending in vowel + consonant

content.subtitle: Form: adverbs
rows: well → **best**, badly → **worst**, early → **earliest**, near → **nearest**, late → **latest**, fast → **fastest**, far → **farthest/furthest**

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: We normally use **the** before a superlative: *That was **the most memorable** concert I've ever been to.*

sections.title: A: Complete with comparative adjectives
sections.instructions: Use the correct comparative form of the adjective in brackets.

items.sentence: This exercise is ...... (easy) than the previous one.
My sister is ...... (tall) than me.
A bike is ...... (cheap) than a car.
Today is ...... (hot) than yesterday.
This film is ...... (interesting) than the one we watched last week.
My new phone is ...... (good) than my old one.
The test was ...... (bad) than I expected.
The station is ...... (far) from here than the airport.

sections.title: B: Complete with superlative adjectives
sections.instructions: Use the correct superlative form of the adjective in brackets.

items.sentence: This is ...... (small) room in the house.
Who is ...... (young) person in your family?
That was ...... (exciting) match of the season.
It's ...... (busy) day of the week for me.
He is ...... (good) player in the team.
This is ...... (bad) café in town.
She bought ...... (cheap) ticket available.
That was ...... (funny) joke I've heard all day.

sections.title: C: Comparatives and superlatives of adverbs
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form.

items.sentence: Nina speaks English ...... (fluently) than I do.
Of all the students, Omar works ...... (carefully).
My brother runs ...... (fast) than me.
Who arrived ...... (early) this morning?
I did ...... (badly) in maths than in science.
Out of everyone in class, Leo answered ...... (well).
Can you drive a bit ...... (slowly)?
She reacted ...... (quickly) than the others.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: This task is **more easy / easier** than I thought.
Mount Everest is **the higher / the highest** mountain in the world.
Your room is **tidier / the tidiest** than mine.
Today is **the hotest / the hottest** day this month.
This route is **less dangerous / the less dangerous** at night.
He finished **more quickly / the most quickly** than the rest of us.
Marta is **the friendliest / friendlier** person in our group.
That explanation was **the least clear / less clear** of all.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake in comparison forms. Find the bold word(s) and write the correct form.

items.sentence: This book is **interestinger** than that one.
She is the **more** intelligent student in class.
My bag is **heavyer** than yours.
He drives **most** carefully than his brother.
This is the **baddest** result we've had.
Today I got up **more early** than usual.
Who sings the **goodest** in your family?
It's the **most cold** day of the year.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same.

items.sentence:
This exam is easier than the last one. **DIFFICULT**
The last exam ...... this one.

No one in the office works as hard as Julia. **HARDEST**
Julia ...... in the office.

My old laptop was not as fast as this one. **FASTER**
This laptop ...... my old one.

Ben is the tallest student in his class. **THAN**
Ben is ...... any other student in his class.

This was the least interesting part of the book. **BORING**
This was ...... part of the book.

I speak English better than my brother. **WELL**
My brother doesn't speak English ...... me.

sections.title: G: Mixed comparison practice
sections.instructions: Each sentence has a mistake in the comparative or superlative form. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: This road is **more wide** than the other one.
She is the **most old** person in the village.
My phone is **more good** than yours.
He runs **more fast** than anyone in the class.
That was the **baddest** film I've ever seen.
This jacket is **more cheap** than the other one.
She is **more tall** than her brother.
It was the **most hot** day of the year.
